_comment: Curated XSS payloads (cross-site scripting). Each payload is injected into the first writable string slot a route exposes: a JSON body field for POST routes, a query-string value for GET routes, or a path parameter where the route has one. PASS = the API does not 500, does not return a stack trace, and does NOT echo the raw payload back unencoded (reflection check). FAIL = stack trace leaked, 500 returned, or raw payload reflected when expected_blocked=true. Payloads sourced from OWASP XSS Filter Evasion + PortSwigger XSS cheat sheet.

payloads.rationale: Canonical <script> injection — first thing any input validator should strip.
Attribute-break + IMG onerror — bypasses naive < / > filters that allow quotes.
javascript: URL — exploitable when the value is reflected into an href.
SVG onload handler — defeats filters that only strip <script>.
iframe srcdoc — encodes the XSS so it bypasses tag-name allowlists.
Body-tag event handler — caught by HTML5 contextual auto-escape but not by older sanitizers.
data: URI carrying base64-encoded <script>alert(1)</script> — bypasses naive http(s):// scheme filters.
Bare event-handler attribute injection — exploits attribute-context reflection.
Autofocus + onfocus chain — no user interaction required.
HTML5 details tag + ontoggle — defeats classic XSS tag allowlists.
OWASP XSS polyglot — single payload that fires in 25+ contexts (HTML, attribute, URL, JS, style).
HTML-entity-encoded script — should round-trip safely as literal text. PASS = server stores or echoes literal '<script>' which is harmless.
Unicode-escaped — if the server unescapes blindly into a JSON string and then renders unsafely, payload activates. PASS = server preserves the escape.
Double-URL-encoded script tag — exploits double-decode bugs in URL parsers.
